narrative: A $4,271.16 underpayment on a $61,014.35 claim was appealed with medical documentation in September 2024, remained under payor review for nearly 6 months with repeated follow-ups, and was finally closed via a $4,136.22 write-off adjustment in April 2025 without evidence of additional payment recovery.

Claim for $1,752.96 was denied as bundled (CO-234, CO-97) by CIGNA; insurer later requested medical records to reconsider but claim remained unresolved for 9 months until a $817.20 adjustment was posted in February 2025, leaving a $935.76 patient responsibility balance.

A claim originally denied in September 2024 for Collector Review was rebilled to Medicare in April 2025 after referral form retrieval, but has been stuck in NPI analyst review since July 2025 with repetitive deferred notes and no resolution for over 8 months.
Claim for $2,344.54 charged on 06/07/2024 to GLOBAL HEALTH was pending EOB posting as of mid-June; full payment of $2,344.54 was received on 06/26/2024, resolving the account.
Charges of $1,856.00 were posted on 2024-05-13 with no subsequent payment, denial, or biller action recorded, leaving the claim pending for over 120 days without human intervention.
Claim was initially not found on file with the payor; after resubmission to the correct address, payment of $4,255.54 was received in full.

Claim for $4,399.76 was paid at $2,711.01; biller pursued underpayment appeal through July–September 2024, but payor upheld the original payment as correct, and the remaining $1,398.13 balance was written off in April 2025.

Multiple CPT lines from the same date of service were denied on 2024-06-14 due to registration/eligibility issues, followed by a new charge posted one week later with no subsequent biller action or resolution.
Claim for $2,655.02 was charged on 2024-05-24, reprocessed by OHCA, and paid in full on 2024-06-05 after biller confirmed payment posting.

Claim was initially not found on file with the payor; after verification of active member eligibility and correction of the payor address, a hardcopy was mailed on 06/28/2024 and full payment of $1,723.80 was received on 08/08/2024.

Claim for DOS 2/21/2024 billed to Aetna for $3,991.00 was partially paid ($572.76) with patient responsibility ($1,885.47) and CPT 80048 denied as mutually exclusive (CO-231); remaining balance of $543.01 was adjusted off as below-threshold inclusive charges.

Claim for $4,887.03 billed 07/12/2024 was processed by Medica on 03/07/2024 with $524.46 applied to patient deductible; remaining balance was transferred to self-pay and written off as patient responsibility.
Claim for DOS 02.29-03.05.24 was not found on Oklahoma payor portal; biller is rebilling with primary EOB and monitoring for processing.

Claim for $5,205.84 to United Healthcare was partially paid at $3,075.84; the remaining $2,130.00 balance for non-covered room charges was denied and appropriately written off as patient responsibility per plan terms.
Claim for DOS 03/06/2024 billed at $3019.00 but expected contracted amount is $1476.42; claim remains pending with payor (BCBS) due to known processing backlog, with no EOB received as of June 2024.

Claim initially denied for wrong primary payor (Medicaid instead of BCBS), then stalled for 3+ months while medical records were uploaded and reviewed, finally adjudicated with partial payment and a denial for missing timely prior authorization.
Claim to AETNA MEDICARE for DOS 3/10/2024–4/12/2024 was paid $7,425.61 with $1,560.00 patient responsibility applied, leaving a $94.49 underpayment that was written off as below-threshold adjustment.

Claim for $2,224.72 (DOS 03/10/2024) billed to BCBS on 07/30/2024 remains pending with no EOB or payment received; payor reported processing backlog as of 07/09/2024, and account has been aging without resolution for over 120 days.
Claim was processed by OHCA with a payment of $290.86 against $2,541.00 in charges, leaving a $12 balance in the self-pay bucket that was adjusted off.

Claim initially denied by primary (Healthcare Highways) as non-covered under member plan; biller identified active secondary Medicaid and rebilled, which paid in full by December 2024, closing the account.
Claim for $11,845.58 (DOS 3/14–3/16/2024) was billed to AETNA in December 2024 and remains pending in the payor system with no payment or denial received after 9+ days.

Claim for DOS 04/04/2024 billed at $45,297.79 but payor Health Choice paid only $13,665.10 against expected contracted amount of $17,866.12, leaving a $4,201.02 underpayment; reconsideration request via provider portal was not found by payor, and formal appeal to PO Box address is now required within 180 days.

Claim for $36,884.73 (DOS 3/15–3/22/2024) to Humana Medicare initially denied for bundled services, then appealed for underpayment; payor reprocessed and ultimately paid $4,898.49 with $2,050 patient responsibility, with balance adjusted off in March 2025 after a 7-month delay.

Claim for $19,324.38 was underpaid by $1,231.00 after initial payment of $8,295.76; multiple appeal attempts over 8+ months were rejected by payor as past timely-filing window, and balance was written off as contractual adjustment.
Claim for $464.61 was transmitted to Oklahoma Complete on 07/15/2024 for DOS 04/08/2024 with no EOB or payment received as of 07/21/2024, and the biller is waiting for payor response.

Claim for $4,061.00 (DOS 04/05/2024) was denied as non-covered under a preventative-only plan, initially appealed but ultimately written off to patient responsibility after discovery that the member's plan covers only preventive and wellness care, not treatment of diagnosed illness.

Claim for DOS 03/26–03/28/2024 was billed at DRG 871 but payor processed and paid at DRG 195 citing insufficient ICD-10 documentation, creating a $4,165.13 underpayment that remains unresolved pending a downgrade letter from the carrier.

Claim for $37,698.92 (DOS 04/29/2024) was underpaid by $3,333.14; an appeal was submitted in September 2024 but the payor could not locate it, and by December 2024 the 180-day timely filing window had expired, forcing a write-off adjustment.

A $37,998 claim underpaid by $3,334 was appealed multiple times over six months, but the payor never received any submission; when timely filing expired (180 days from 06/22/2024), the balance was written off as uncollectible.

A $11.8K charge from April 2024 was billed to Humana on 1/28/2025 with multiple offsetting adjustments posted before any payment arrived; a $6.7K payment posted 2/19/2025 leaves the claim still pending with significant unresolved balance and a large discrepancy between the biller's stated claim amount ($43.6K) and the ledger charge ($11.8K).

Claim for $6,838.00 DOS 4/9/2024 billed to Oklahoma Complete Health was denied as CO-22 (may be covered by another plan); biller initiated a fax request to the payor on 7/28/2024 and is awaiting response.
Claim for $3,041.00 was created on 2024-08-26 and is awaiting initial processing and payor response.

Claim initially denied for submission/billing error (CO-16), resubmitted and partially paid with CPT modifiers denied (CO-236); remaining small underpayment of $92.03 was written off as below-threshold.

Claim for $2,506.92 (DOS 04/22/2024) was underpaid at $0 with $810.96 patient responsibility; appeal submitted September 2024 to payor but remains unresolved after 8 months with no response from insurer.

Claim for $3,416.00 (DOS 04/29/2024–05/25/2024) was denied for lack of prior authorization; biller identified missing auth in Epic, obtained authorization, and routed to supervisor for corrected claim submission to the payor's mailing address.

A split-bill Humana Medicare claim for April 2024 inpatient stay was resubmitted in December 2024; one bill type (137) paid partially without denial, but bill type 127 was denied for CPT J1815 as non-covered, and the account was escalated to supervisor discussion log in February 2025 pending resolution.
Claim for DOS 4/14–4/16/2024 was transmitted to Humana on 12/16/2024 for an inpatient case with TC charge of $1,448.00; biller is monitoring for payor response.

Claim for DOS 5/6/2024 cycled through COB and coding denials over 11 months, ultimately processed as secondary Medicaid with $1,182.56 payment and $501.57 balance adjustment after verification that expected contractual amount was received.

A $10,069.46 claim for DOS 04/19/2024 has cycled through multiple payors (Aetna MCD, UHC, Aetna Better Health) over 11 months, repeatedly denied for missing primary EOB and revenue codes, with hardcopy resubmissions mailed in September 2024 and June 2025 but no resolution received as of July 2025.

Claim for $43,929.31 (DOS 05/22–05/24/2024) billed to Health Choice was paid $26,231.46; a medical-records denial was initially issued but records were submitted 09/16/2024; an underpayment dispute on CPT 27130 was escalated 10/22/2024; the remaining balance of $1,622.84 was written off under contractual adjustment on 12/27/2024 after the payor upheld the denial.

Claim for DOS 7/10/2024 was initially denied by primary HEALTHSMART for missing group number; after group number was added and claim resubmitted, it was then billed to secondary Humana MCd on 12/11/2024 and is currently pending response.

Claim for $2,069.00 (DOS 04/30/2024) was incorrectly billed to MANGUM POLICE DEPT instead of the appropriate payor, identified as out-of-scope for the current workflow and returned without collection action.

Claim billed to BCBS for DOS 4/30/2024 with $1,312.91 balance remains unpaid and denied pending records; reprocessing request sent in September after records were submitted in June with no payor response.

OHCA processed a UB claim for $2654.51, paid $458.39, and denied multiple revenue codes and CPT codes (J1100, S0028) as inactive/non-covered, with an adjustment applied via Epic.

Claim for $7,120.81 was billed to Aetna as secondary but Aetna processed it as primary with no COB update; after failed appeal to correct the coordination-of-benefits conflict, a $3,442.00 balance was written off to self-pay due to unresolvable payor positioning.

Humana recouped a previously paid claim totaling $12,251.14 because the member has WEB TPA as primary insurance; the claim was then billed to the patient after COB verification revealed no active secondary coverage details.

A $3,951 claim for DOS 5/17/2024 was not found in the payor portal; the biller rebilled via hardcopy mail to the correct address in October 2024 and is now waiting for EOB response, but the account has been open and unresolved for over 120 days.

Claim for $3,138 DOS 5/15/2024 was processed with $2,153.50 applied to patient deductible, but member ID mismatches between EOB and Epic have blocked posting for over 9 months while payor remains behind on funding.

A $65,210 claim for DOS 06/06/2024 was underpaid by $4,240.66; after three months of appeal attempts through the payor portal and phone calls, the 180-day appeal window expired and the balance was written off as uncollectible.

A $2,450.50 claim for DOS 5/16/2024 was billed to Oklahoma Complete Health as secondary insurance in January 2025, denied for timely filing (past the payor's filing window), and is currently under additional review pending payor decision.

Claim for $2,843.00 was billed to Kempton, received payment of $1,231.87 with $307.97 patient responsibility, and the remaining balance was accepted and adjusted per Medicare rates with no denials encountered.

Claim for $3,007.79 (DOS 5/23–5/24/2024) was transmitted to Aetna Medicaid on 10/16/2024 and remains pending with no payment or denial as of late October 2024, but the service date is now over 5 months old.

Humana Medicaid paid $5,702.96 then recouped the claim citing missing or invalid HCPCS codes on revenue codes 250, 272, and 278; biller is awaiting guidance on corrected coding resubmission for the $41,467.29 charge from 6/11/2024.

Account contains two separate DOS periods with distinct denials: a May 2024 inpatient stay underpaid due to DRG downgrade from 872 to 603 (payor refusing reprocessing), and a January 2024 claim denied as duplicate with no active member coverage, both requiring manual appeal or resubmission.

Claim for $5,231.70 was denied by Cigna Orchestrate HR on 10/30/2024 for timely filing (CO-29), but biller disputes the denial citing plan-year-specific filing windows; after multiple calls and EOB review, the balance was adjusted off as a write-off under the timely filing code once the appeal window closed.

Medicare reprocessed and recouped a $1,219.68 payment for a 6/6/24 claim due to overlapping inpatient dates at another facility (5/16/24–7/3/24), and the biller deferred resolution 30 days pending ISR review of why the high-dollar reimbursement was not flagged on the overlap spreadsheet.

A $43,414.56 claim was denied because the patient's Title 19 coverage did not become effective until 6/11/24, making the 6/10/24 service date ineligible; the balance was later transferred to patient responsibility as mental/substance health coverage only.

Claim for DOS 06/11–06/12/2024 was initially denied for medical necessity and coding errors, then later denied for bundled/inclusive CPT codes (96360–96379, 96521–96523); biller accepted Cigna's policy determination and wrote off $2,934 in charges via contractual adjustment on 10/01/2024.

Workers' compensation claim for $1,728.00 (DOS 06/13/2024) billed to Sedgwick was denied by adjustor Catheline Moore in August 2024 and remains unresolved after multiple unsuccessful contact attempts spanning over 16 months, with the claim ultimately forwarded to patient responsibility in February 2026.

Outpatient claim for $7,473 was recouped by the payor due to CO-60 (proximity to inpatient services), and the balance was legitimately adjusted after verification that the inpatient claim was paid under a different patient account.

Claim for $4,686 DOS 07/12/2024 was initially processed by Epic Health Choice with $1,626.61 patient deductible responsibility, then submitted to VA which rejected it citing non-coverage of deductibles, and the balance was written off as patient self-pay.

Claim for $2,366.00 DOS 07/24/2024 was initially billed to HealthChoice but member's policy terminated 06/30/2024; biller discovered alternate primary insurance (Oklahoma Complete Health) and is rebilling to the correct payor within the 365-day timely filing window.

Claim for DOS 8/20/2024 was underpaid by $811.40 according to the EOB, but the 180-day timely filing window for appeal expired before the underpayment appeal could be submitted, resulting in a write-off of the remaining balance.

Claim denied for missing HCPCS codes; medical records and claim review form were mailed to BCBS on 12/03/2024 under certified mail, and the account is awaiting payor response as of 12/17/2024 (111 days post-DOS).

Claim for $14,488.88 was initially sent to the wrong payor (Oklahoma), denied, and has been resubmitted to the correct payor where it remains pending verification.

Cigna denied therapeutic infusion codes (96360–96379, 96521–96523) as bundled/inclusive per CO-97, and the biller accepted the denial and wrote off the $1,062 balance as non-separately payable under contractual policy.
